preview.tournamentOverview: Aronimink Golf Club hosts the PGA Championship with a layout that heavily favors approach play and total strokes gained. The course places a premium on iron play and ball-striking consistency, while putting and driving accuracy carry less weight. This major tests players' ability to control shots into firm greens and avoid bogeys on a demanding track.
preview.modelExplainer: SG Approach receives the highest allocation at 30.7%, followed by SG Total at 22.8% and SG Off-the-Tee at 16.2%. SG Around-the-Green is weighted at 11.9%, while SG Putting, Driving Accuracy, and Bogey Avoidance each fall below 9%. These weights prioritize players who excel with irons and overall ball-striking over specialists in short game or putting.
preview.pickApproach: The model ranks players by combining course-specific weights with baseline power rankings to identify value. Top selections like Jake Knapp and Scottie Scheffler lead due to strong approach and total strokes gained metrics. Bettors should target golfers who post elite SG Approach and SG Total numbers while avoiding those carried only by putting or around-the-green stats.
